title: tithymalus chamaebuxus
intro: Tithymalus chamaebuxus, also known as the dwarf or wild boxwood, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Euphorbia family. Traditionally, the plant's sap has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tithymalus chamaebuxus include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support wound healing and skin protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by crushing the leaves or stems and using them in ointments, salves, or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
